programme.title: Diagnosis Murder

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: The body of a celebrity is found at a party and the team discovers links with a mysterious gambling ring offering participants the chance to bet on the dates of famous people's deaths. Further investigation leads the officers to an offshore operation producing high-quality counterfeit banjoes. The forensic spin-off show's 100th episode, starring David Caruso and Emily Procter

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A cook from a US Navy destroyer tells McGee and Gibbs he believes someone is stalking him with the intention of trying to make him poison the crew, and a picture on a stolen phone leads the team to a group testing the psychological effects of surveillance. Meanwhile, Richard Parsons from the Inspector General's office closes in on Ziva as he investigates the death of Ilan Bodnar, the man who killed her father. Guest starring Colin Hanks (Gus Grimly in the Fargo TV series)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony finds himself in a difficult position when his identity is stolen. Worse yet, multiple fraudsters make use of his status as a special agent to blackmail US senators, and as the chain of events begins to spiral out of control, it is left to the real Dinozzo to bring the perpetrators to justice. Naval crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The family of a child who finds a gun suspected of being a murder weapon at a naval base comes under suspicion. Crime drama, starring Pauley Perrette and Mark Harmon

programme.desc: Gibbs teams up with his old friend Senior FBI Agent TC Fornell after two British prisoners, including a former spy, escape captivity and flee to the US via a shipping container. programme.desc: The team investigates when Navy Seal Chad McBride's wife receives a package from Riyadh in Saudi Arabia containing her husband's severed head, which Palmer discovers contains very high levels of radiation. Meanwhile, Richard Parsons tries to get Gibbs to take responsibility for the many unnecessary deaths for which he holds the NCIS team accountable

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A 14-year-old boy manages to foil a home invasion, but when Gibbs is called in to investigate, he suspects the teenager may be withholding crucial information. Meanwhile, Director Vance and FBI Agent Fornell travel to London as part of the ongoing case involving the escaped British spies

Detective drama, guest starring James McAvoy, with Nathaniel Parker and Sharon Small

At the same time, Grace receives threats and a macabre package - a jar of pickled, severed fingers. Sue Johnston and Trevor Eve star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team reopens the case of criminal Mark Johnson, who was killed in a fire at London's Union club in 1996. The original investigation was hampered by silence among witnesses and associates, but with new information suggesting the blaze was a targeted arson attack, the detectives hope they will have more success. Drama, guest starring Roy Marsden, Martin Marquez and Tom Watt

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The team reopens a murder case Spencer worked on while he was a policeman at the Atomic Energy Commission, in which two anti-nuclear campaigners were killed. With definite DNA evidence at their disposal, Boyd and his staff are confident about making a conviction

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: An abandoned car leads Callum and Will to a woman caught up in a tragic love triangle in which jealousy explodes into violence as the aggrieved husband stabs his rival and then threatens to kill himself. Meanwhile, a man trying to obtain his birth certificate at a register office discovers his identity has been stolen, alerting Sally and Tony to an extensive benefit fraud scam. Emma Samms and Stephen McGann guest star

programme.desc: Andreena and partner Germaine feel they have outgrown their home in Walthamstow. They want to sample a simpler life in Kent, but have to convince their daughter Renee to join them

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Detectives trail a burglar who is amongst the most callous they have ever encountered, and a trusted employee runs out of luck when she is caught stealing scratch cards

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Danny Sebastian and David Harper help two teams scour Nottingham racecourse for items to take to auction, while Natasha Raskin Sharp finds out about one of the city's great authors

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Max and Miranda investigate a missing person case, during which they dig up a series of historical murders linking a mass grave to a washed-up private eye. Phil Daniels guest stars

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: The penultimate leg of Si King and Dave Myers' European baking tour takes them to France. Travelling from Lyon to Carcassonne, they learn the secrets of teeth-pulling nougat, go truffle hunting and meet up with Dave's old flatmate, whom he has not seen for 30 years. Culinary delights en route include tarte aux pralines, a savoury-stuffed brioche, apricot tarte Tatin and, of course, the traditional French baguette

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple from Northampton find a new home in Norfolk with spectacular views. He also helps a local chef to prepare some seasonal shellfish caught on the coast

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: The return of the programme celebrating inspirational farming families and the rural events where they showcase their hard work. Poultry farmers Nick and Jacob and organic and heritage fruit and vegetable farmer Patrick display their wares at Alexandra Palace Farmers' Market

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers bid against each other for items brought in by the public including a set of soda syphons, a collection of football figures and a curious and shocking machine

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda's suspicions grow after Max's shocking announcement and she is determined to get the truth. Denise accuses Lucas of manipulating Chelsea. Honey suggests talking to the kids about her and Jay, but is angry when Billy, still sulking, shows up late

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: The Darwin team struggles to save patients as the hospital goes into lockdown. Cameron faces being exposed as completely out of his depth, and is forced to phone Nicky for advice in theatre. Alex tries to help a stressed Donna to remain calm, stirring up some unresolved feelings, while Dom decides to return to the world of internet dating

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Edinburgh, ambulance crew Stephen and Gwion are called to a festival worker who needs treatment for a reaction to a bee sting. An elderly patient with labyrinthitis has become dizzy and needs to be taken to hospital. A sepsis case at a GP surgery needs emergency treatment and a fast transfer to hospital.

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Mary Berry challenges the celebrity cooks to prepare the perfect breakfast for houseguests. Two of the contestants adopt a Mexican theme, pitting quesadillas against breakfast burritos, while another presents the judges with an edible crossword. Mary and fellow judges Angela Hartnett and Chris Bavin each select their favourite dish, but even this is no protection against being eliminated from the contest. Claudia Winkleman presents

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, featuring Manchester United v Southampton, Sheffield United v West Bromwich Albion, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Arsenal and Newcastle United v Crystal Palace

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic 4x100m gold medallist Mark Lewis-Francis, swimming great Rebecca Adlington and Scottish internationals in football and rugby union respectively Charlie Adam and John Barclay join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones return with the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell check out a maisonette in London, a flat in Shirley in the West Midlands, and a triangular piece of land in Blean near Canterbury

programme.title: Wanted: A Simple Life
programme.desc: Hannah Lemon feels ready for a new and wild adventure away from the London scene, and considers making a move to the Isle of Skye in the Scottish Highlands

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Two cars are stolen from a family's driveway, and fly tippers leave clues amongst the rubbish dumped at a beauty spot. Presented by Dom Littlewood

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Catherine Southon lend their antiques expertise to two teams in Lewes, East Sussex, as they compete to make a profit at auction. Presenter Christina Trevanion also pays a visit to the nearby Brighton Toy Museum

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: After the death of famous food critic Oliver Barker, Max and Miranda embark on a culinary journey through Mallorca on the hunt for his murderer

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: The duo's baking odyssey ends in northern Spain, where treats include local bread pan rustico, seafood empanadas and a spelt sausage roll packed with chorizo

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton looks for property on Anglesey for a couple who have a £475,000 budget, and also learns about the importance of preserving the island's ancient woodland

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two families of pig farmers are hoping to turn their dreams into a reality and win the top prizes with their precious livestock at the Royal Norfolk Show

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers bid against each other on items including a vintage train set, a set of mid-century chairs, a 1940s style microphone, and an old butchers block

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes Yorkshireman Ged, who has brought in a classic piece of industrial furniture saved from the scrap heap. Henrie wants to sell a silver and mahogany Chinese inkwell from the early 19th century. And Molly from Manchester is selling a a watchman's timepiece

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team of experts bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Guitar expert Julyan Wallis is presented with a cherished 1960s jazz guitar which has been left in a broken state for decades. Meanwhile, wood restoration expert Will Kirk has his work cut out when is presented with a handmade veneered card table. Later, the experts turn their attention to bookmakers bag and a Victorian clockwork pigeon

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The remaining seven celebrity home cooks are put through their paces once more. First, Mary Berry challenges the contestants to make their ultimate burger and chips, before Chris Bavin reveals frozen peas as the secret ingredient that the celebrities must make the star of the dish for his rustle up challenge on day two. The three judges must then decide who will be cooking again in this week's eliminator challenge, following one of Angela's toughest recipes to date

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of tonight's Premier League fixtures, which were Burnley v Manchester City, Fulham v Leicester City, Leeds United v Everton, Aston Villa v West Ham United, and Liverpool v Brighton & Hove Albion. Plus, a round-up of last night's goals and talking points

programme.title: Fight for First: Excel Esports
programme.desc: Excel continue their battle for contention, but with cracks appearing in the team, will they be able to beat Schalke 04? The players open up about what it's like being in the public eye at such a young age, and how the online echo chamber affects their mental health and their careers. Plus, League of Legends commentator 'Frosk' talks about being a woman in a male-dominated industry

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: An update on a four-storey property in Ramsgate, Kent, that Dion Dublin first visited back in 2017. Plus, the progress of a two-bedroom semi in London and a bungalow in Bilston in the West Midlands

programme.title: Wanted: A Simple Life
programme.desc: The Elliotts are part of Belfast's showbiz scene but Pauline wants a quieter life, and hopes the Causeway Coast will convince husband Robin the time has come for a quieter life

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A dog walker's cruel actions spark outrage from animal lovers, a bartender faces a man stealing her tips and a pair of jokers are caught taking a bus for a joyride

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts from Southwell Racecourse in Nottinghamshire, where experts Thomas Forrester and Richard Madley help competing teams bag the best buys to make a profit at auction in neighbouring Lincolnshire. Anita also learns about a Lancaster bomber's restoration

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: A Real Mallorca player starts receiving death threats in light of rumours that he might be transferring to another club, so Max and Miranda are assigned to protect him - leading them into a world of dodgy deals and illicit affairs in the world of top-flight football

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King travel around Asia exploring the roots of some of their favourite dishes from the continent. They begin in Hong Kong, where they learn about the versatility of the wok in Cantonese cuisine at a street-food stall, and watch noodle-making rodeo-style

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin embarks on a house-hunting expedition in north Wales with a couple who have a £300,000 budget and a wish list including a craft room and community close by. Away from his quest, Jonnie cannot resist trying out Snowdonia's latest outdoor activity, catching a wave at the world's first artificial surf lagoon

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Former police officer Huw Murphy and the Harries family go up against each other with their beloved shire horses at the 37th South Wales Shire Horse Society Show

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers bid against each other on a childhood Viewmaster collection, a set of art deco candle holders and an apothecary box. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Alex Scott host the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mick is worried for Tina and tells Linda that nothing makes sense, while Kathy pleads with Phil to help find Ian. Denise confronts Lucas, convinced he has let daughter Chelsea down yet again. Billy is hurt that Jay is able to buy his kids new trainers and becomes desperate to find a new job

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: The first set of quarter-finalists take on a traditional dog groom when they gently removing the dead hairs from the coat of one of the UK's oldest breeds, the Welsh terrier, before producing a very modern look for the Dogwalk that requires smiley faces, flared legs and little outfits. Plus, in a new challenge, the contestants' knowledge of crossbreeds is put to the test as they try to identify a dog's parents. Presented by Sheridan Smith, with judges Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: When renowned concert pianist Pasha Verdinikov is shot dead during an apparent burglary, Neville is quick to conclude that the scene was staged and Pasha was murdered. But the four people under suspicion - the victim's family and domestic workers - all have seemingly solid alibis. However, matters are complicated when the discovery of new evidence links the crime to a case from 20 years ago. First of a two-part story, starring Ralf Little and Josephine Jobert

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Supermarket Secrets
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace examines the ways supermarkets fight for customers' loyalty, and visits a farm claiming to be using technology to produce healthy chicken at a low cost. Elsewhere, Gregg meets Asda's head of fruit and vegetables to find out why it is a challenge to remove plastic packaging from products, and Nikki Fox sees how they are using a virtual supermarket to see what appeals to us most

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wanted: A Simple Life
programme.desc: Chris Bavin helps families sample rural living to see if it could bring them a new lease of life. A couple plan to move from Cheshire to the Dorset coast

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: The programme meets Melissa and Kenny, who breed and train sheepdogs, while Billy and his nephew Jock are hoping for success at the sheepdog trials

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: The return of the documentary about the county's fishing communities, beginning in the popular tourist resort of Newquay as restrictions begin to ease after the coronavirus lockdown. It is not a moment too soon for the town's fishermen, most of whom have been tied up for months, and with lost time to catch up on, and livelihoods on the line, the fleet is steaming into a summer like no other

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: Mark Wright will bring fitness to secondary kids as he leads a daily dose of workout sessions, joined by a host of his celebrity friends

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Bitesize Daily for Secondary
programme.desc: Daily show to support home learning. Topics include grammar and expanding your vocabulary

programme.title: Black and British: A Forgotten History
programme.desc: Continuing his exploration of black British history, historian David Olusoga investigates further into the stories of thousands of Africans transported into slavery by the British, looking back at key historical events that turned Britain into the biggest slave-trading nation in the world, and shaped the future of Britain for black people. David learns about conflicting English laws that were implemented against the slaves and discovers more about the people who challenged these laws and contributed to the end of slavery

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Paul Hayes and John Cameron visit an auction in Warrington and go head to head over a cameo brooch, and Paul resorts to sneaky tactics to hide items from his rival

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals how reptiles and amphibians, including the pebble toad and Jesus Christ lizard, conquer their shortcomings by using tricks and strategies to avoid predators. He also explores how chameleons can achieve a high level of accuracy with their muscle-propelled, extendible tongues, and finds out how a 9ft-long Komodo dragon can bring down a huge water buffalo

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Womanising male Houdini is on the prowl - and female Flower is ready for breeding. But it remains to be seen whether the two hit it off. Pregnant Kinkajoo emerges as the group's dominant female and Mozart helps Carlos when he begins to suffer from an infected wound. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Ragley Hall, Warwickshire, antiques experts Christina Trevanion and Charlie Ross find a selection of antiques and collectibles to take to auction. Christina gets to grips with a weighty jardiniere, Charlie tests out a horse-measuring stick on its owner and presenter Paul Martin steps into the world of folk art

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Raj Bisram, Josie Lawrence, Mark Watson and Laura Whitmore go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: The chef takes a culinary tour of France to discover what Britain's neighbours have to teach us. He begins his trip in Dieppe, where he prepares freshly caught turbot, forages for sea herbs along the Bay of the Somme, and cooks a seafood gratin. He then heads for southern Champagne, a region that was a favourite of Renoir

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: Alan Carr hosts a new series of the interior design challenge, in which 10 aspiring designers compete to redesign Oxfordshire show home. Michelle Ogundehin assesses their efforts, and is joined in the first episode by guest judge Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen

programme.title: This World
programme.desc: The second part of this two-part documentary explores the initial outbreak of Covid-19 in the US. When Covid-19 first arrived on American soil, US federal health authorities were seemingly unaware of how transmissible the virus truly was. This account chronicles 54 days in which the United States made crucial decisions that would severely impact the spread of the virus. Featuring interviews with public health officials and scientists who had already concluded that the virus had global pandemic potential, who were confronted with a failing diagnostic test and a president who appeared to repeatedly undermine the true threat of the virus

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig asks questions on the theme of rest and recreation this week, with Stephen K Amos, Susan Calman, Lou Sanders and Alan Davies trying to come up with interesting answers - while avoiding the most obvious ones

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: DeLorean: Back from the Future
programme.desc: Documentary charting rise and fall of John Z DeLorean and his doomed attempt to build the sports car of the future in 1980s war-torn Belfast. The programme features rare and unseen footage filmed by Oscar winning directors DA Pennebaker and Chris Hegedus, offering the first in-depth psychological profile of DeLorean

programme.title: Silenced: The Hidden Story of Disabled Britain
programme.desc: Cerrie Burnell presents a history of disabled people's struggle for human rights in Britain. The film looks back to the policies in place in Victorian workhouses, uncovering the original records of an institution created to segregate disabled people and stop them from having children. She also shares inspiring stories of pioneering campaigners for social change, and looks at the challenges still to be faced in the future

programme.title: Targeted: The Truth about Disability Hate Crime
programme.desc: Disabled filmmaker Richard Butchins' brutally honest film explores the abuse and aggression faced by disabled people in everyday life, from verbal insults to violent physical attacks. The film features a wide range of testimony from survivors themselves as they share their own experiences of the abuse they have faced

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham catches up on the action from the live cameras in the New Forest and on the River Ness in Scotland, while Iolo Williams follows a feral flock of barnacle geese. Elsewhere, cameras delve below the surface of Loch Carron to see the amazing brittlestars who thrive there in winter

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A distinctive sticker on a laptop finally links a man to a string of burglaries that had terrified a community, and shiny copper hair dye puts a dangerous armed robber behind bars

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents from Oswestry Showground in Shropshire, where Kate Bliss and Ben Cooper are helping today's reds and blues. Natasha also talks to Ben about his family

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Weekly magazine for the deaf and hard of hearing. With voiceover

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson's grandmother's recipe for creme caramel provides the inspiration for a simple but elegant dessert of caramel custard. She then rifles through her pasta collection to find the ideal shape to go with her spicy sauce made from Italian 'nduja and cavolo nero, followed by a visit to the gadget cupboard for an electric potato masher to prepare her brown butter colcannon, the perfect match to a dish of black pudding meatballs in tomato sauce.

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: Mark Wright will bring fitness to secondary kids as he leads a daily dose of workout sessions, joined by a host of his celebrity friends

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Bitesize Daily for Secondary
programme.desc: Topics include Spanish, maths, and the particle model in chemistry

programme.title: Black and British: A Forgotten History
programme.desc: Historian David Olusoga continue his exploration of black British history. Focusing on the Victorian era, David explores the moral crusade against slavery within this period of time and key moments that helped to shift the position of slavery within Britain. He discovers about mill workers of Rochdale that stood in solidarity with enslaved Africans in the American South, how Queen Victoria came to have a black god-daughter, and looks back at the victims of a tragedy in Jamaica

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Paul Hayes and John Cameron search for bargains at Ardingly Antiques Fair, where John hopes to get lucky with a wishing well and a painting takes Paul on a Shakespearean journey

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals how mammals dominate the planet and survive in extreme habitats. In the Antarctic, a lone Weddell seal wears its teeth down keeping an ice hole open to catch fish for its pup. In warmer climes, the gerbil-like rufous sengi demonstrates impressive speed to avoid reptilian predators, giant bats feast on fruit trees in Zambia and male humpback whales battle for a female

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Flower's latest offspring emerge for the first time, and trouble looms when a group of Zappa males pays a visit to the Whiskers' burrow

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Charles Hanson are in Margam Country Park in south Wales to value items brought in by the public, while presenter Paul Martin finds out about the link between the estate and the nearby Port Talbot steelworks.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Raj Bisram, Josie Lawrence, Mark Watson and Laura Whitmore test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: The chef continues his 'journey without maps', leaving the main routes and tourist hotspots behind to embark on a personal road trip along picturesque and remote backroads. Here, he heads through France's eastern border regions of Alsace and Jura - famous for Comte cheese and home to his favourite French dish of choucroute garni. He then begins to head inland, visiting Burgundy for a memorable glass of Auxey-Duresses wine, and sampling oeuf en meurette with a difference. Following a memorable Sunday lunch near the French/Swiss border, he is inspired to recreate a traditional blueberry tart

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Matt, Dani and Murray in the Lake District await the arrival of over 1,100 lambs in six weeks, but some early lambs make an unscheduled appearance. Meanwhile in Northumberland, shepherdess Emma Gray and firefighter husband Ewan are preparing for the Whitebred Shorthorn pedigree bull sale in Carlisle, and in Cumbria, father and daughter team Frank and Georgia are nearing the end of the birthing season for their 120 goats

programme.title: Breathe (2017)
programme.desc: The inspirational true story of Robin and Diana Cavendish and the devastating polio diagnosis that changed their lives forever. When Robin is abruptly paralysed and reliant on a mechanical respirator, he and his wife endeavour to conquer the limitations they are faced with, and devote their lives to help other patients struck down with the disease. Biographical drama, starring Andrew Garfield and Claire Foy

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Incredible Journeys with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon Reeve presents highlights of his journeys around the world, and catches up with the people he met, including a Burmese undercover human rights campaigner, a homeless woman living in a Hollywood railway bridge, and a 10 year-old boy he met working in terrible conditions in a glass factory in Bangladesh

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at how the uneven distribution of fresh water around the globe has given rise to an incredible diversity of species and habits, from the driest desert to the lushest tropical rainforest. Featuring a colony of ants banding together into a raft every time its home in the Amazon floods, a rain frog that manages to eke out an existence in one of the world's driest habitats on Earth, and the last wild camels that survive the Gobi Desert's bitter winters by eating snow that blows in from Siberia

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Reports on the wonderful wildlife and seasonal beauty to be found in winter. Chris Packham is in the New Forest looking at the shape of nests and different species of bee, Iolo Williams looks at tree creepers and Gillian Burke reports from the Beaver Project in Cornwall. Cameraman Richard Taylor-Jones tells the story of the Oare Marshes on the River Swale, which has gone from a site making gunpowder to a wildlife haven

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a con woman's fake claims of having cancer were exposed by her distinctive wallpaper, and the hidden compartment in a car that brought a gang defrauding charities to justice

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Weekly magazine for the deaf and hard of hearing. With voiceover

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wanted: A Simple Life
programme.desc: Hannah Lemon feels ready for a new and wild adventure away from the London scene, and considers making a move to the Isle of Skye in the Scottish Highlands

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple wanting to uproot from Essex and find a modest cottage in Cornwall to call their forever home. The two nurses have a budget of £340,000

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith presents as four more professional dog groomers are put to the test. First up in the Breed Makeover round are four English cocker spaniels, as the contestants try to tame the gun dogs' wig and furnishings. They are then challenged to create a tricky spiral around the coats of four frizzy-haired models, before parading them on the dogwalk, where their owners see the results for the first time. Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor judge their efforts and decide who is going through to the next round

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: Mark Wright will bring fitness to secondary kids as he leads a daily dose of workout sessions, joined by a host of his celebrity friends

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Bitesize Daily for Secondary
programme.desc: Topics include 2D shapes, Standard English and Humanism

programme.title: Black and British: A Forgotten History
programme.desc: Historian David Olusoga's exploration of the enduring relationship between Britain and people whose origins lie in Africa concludes. This time, he profiles the three African kings who took a stand against the machinations of an empire, along with a crooner whose voice was considered 'irresistible'. He also investigates a history of race riots in Liverpool, and outlines how the sense of a black British identity has been shaped by various forces throughout the span of the 20th century

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Paul Hayes and John Cameron face off at their final battle - the showdown - and with half their items going under the hammer in Brighton, it's anyone's game. Paul unearths a genuine Roman pot, and John tries to carve out a profit with a totem pole, but there can be only one winner

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: The focus turns to fish, the most diverse vertebrates on the planet, made up of more than 28,000 species. Off the coast of South Australia, sea dragons pair up for courtship, engaging in a mirror dance before the eggs are laid on the male's tail. Gobies climb up the vertical rocks of Hawaiian waterfalls to access secluded pools at the summit, and flying fish use their elongated pectoral fins to escape from predators. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Punk takes charge of the Zappas and immediately begins planning an attack on the vulnerable Whiskers

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The team heads to Sandon Hall in Staffordshire, where Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson find some treasures, while Paul Martin hears about pioneering local woman

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Raj Bisram, Josie Lawrence, Mark Watson and Laura Whitmore test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: Much of lockdown has been spent lamenting the loss of attending live performance across sport, music and theatre. Mary finds out why audiences matter and asks what role they play. She asks sports stars whether the loss of a crowd affects their performance, discusses the changing role of the spectator across history with Kwame Kwei-Armah, Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Kirsty Sedgman, and attends her first Twitter listening party - a new way for musicians to reach their audience. She also talks to Tim Burgess of the Charlatans, who came up with the idea, and the Rev Richard Coles, former keyboard player with 1980s band the Communards

programme.title: Nigella: At My Table
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson rustles up some of her favourite dishes to share around the table, including a lunch menu featuring herbed leg of lamb served with garlicky roast potatoes with oregano and feta, followed by a soft and rich chocolate olive oil mousse. She also prepares coconut shrimp with turmeric yoghurt, and a ginger and walnut carrot cake

programme.title: Your Garden Made Perfect
programme.desc: Following on from the innovative series Your Home Made Perfect, Angela Scanlon returns with this show that offers the same hi-tech makeover technology to outdoor spaces. First up are Demi and Laura from Hertfordshire, who lead a busy family life, meaning they haven't had time to deal with their lacklustre, L-shaped plot. Garden designers Helen Elks-Smith and Manoj Malde pitch for the chance to redesign the site - demonstrating what they would do with virtual reality presentations that put the couple right at the heart of their new, improved outdoor havens. But which one will they choose?

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: A selection of the best moments from the series. Sandi Toksvig asks questions connected to the letter R, as Alan Davies and the guest panellists try to come up with interesting, point-worthy answers - all the while avoiding the most obvious ones

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as Maisie Adam, Angela Barnes, Ed Gamble, Glenn Moore and Ahir Shah join regular panellist Hugh Dennis to take a comic look back on the latest events in news and politics. Once again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Margaret Thatcher's last full year as British prime minister contrasted with global political change, while in the music world Pete Waterman's final year of chart domination heralded the dawn of Madchester. Some female artists brought attitude and power to 1989, while an unlikely duet between a 1960s legend and an 1980s icon proved a chart-topper and Jive Bunny kept the less discerning happy. As far as Top of the Pops was concerned, Radio 1's old guard was stood down as a team of fresh-faced recruits helmed the show. Contributors include Jason Donovan, Lisa Stansfield, Shaun Ryder, Chris Rea, Marc Almond, Sharleen Spiteri, Jazzie B and Jenny Powell

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Incredible Journeys with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon Reeve recalls some of the most dangerous experiences of his travels, from coming under fire in war-torn Mogadishu to squaring off with a female wrestler in Mexico City. Among the past destinations featured are a police cell in the far east of Russia, and the programme also looks back at his encounters with ambulance crews in San Salvador and anti-smuggling police in Italy

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Over the past 12 months, a team from Imperial College London, in collaboration with the BBC, have surveyed the mental health of over 350,000 people across the UK during the Covid-19 pandemic. Now, clinical psychologist Professor Tanya Byron joins former England footballer Alex Scott to discover how the latest science can help us gain greater control over our state of mind and improve mental health and wellbeing

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Megan McCubbin joins Chris Packham in the New Forest, catching up on the drama showing up on the live cameras at his badger sett and the spawning salmon in the River Ness. Chris also takes a look at the reasons why waders bob. Meanwhile, Iolo Williams is at the Centre for Alternative Technology in Wales, hoping to see some amazing sights and Gillian Burke is at the Beaver Project in Cornwall

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Made in Britain
programme.desc: Fred meets fellow traction engine enthusiasts in Staffordshire and visits an old friend who is restoring a triple expansion engine at Bratch Pumping Station. He also takes a tour of the trains on the Severn Valley railway and learns about the history of mining in the Black Country at the Living Museum

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints the scene from a cabin, hidden in the middle of a plush green forest and surrounded by the sounds of nature

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: In the hope of avoiding a fuss, Clegg plans a quiet birthday lunch at a nice hotel - but his pals are sure to throw a few spanners in the works. Long-running comedy set in Yorkshire, from the pen of Roy Clarke. Peter Sallis and Frank Thornton star

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Matter of Loaf and Death
programme.desc: The duo open their own bakery, Top Bun, and business is soon booming - not least because a 'cereal killer' is targeting all the rival bakers in town. Even better, Wallace has caught the eye of local beauty Piella Bakewell. But Gromit is more concerned that his master might become the killer's next victim. With the voices of Peter Sallis and Sally Lindsay

programme.title: Life of a Mountain: A Year on Scafell Pike
programme.desc: Documentary charting England's highest peak as it is transformed by the seasons - seen through the eyes of those who climb it for pleasure, those who try to protect its slopes, and the Cumbrian farmers who work in the surrounding valleys and fells surrounding the Lake District National Park. Contributions from the British Mountaineering Council and National Trust volunteers highlight the importance of maintaining the landscape for future generations

programme.title: The Great Mountain Sheep Gather
programme.desc: Documentary following a shepherd's journey from summit to valley as he leads his sheep off Scafell Pike, reflecting on life caring for his flock in this rugged landscape. Maxine Peake reads specially commissioned poetry written by Mark Pajak to provide a counterpoint to the shepherd's insights throughout the film.

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Josephine heads back to Paris with Youssef, but his brother Souleymane is now in grave danger, while Judge Vargas, who was in charge of Gilou's infiltration, decides to cut his losses. But what does this mean for Gilou?

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Last-minute changes on the day of the hold-up cause chaos and the police's meticulously planned intervention is thrown into disarray. As the situation becomes more and more dangerous, Laure does all she can to get Gilou out to safety, but her options are limited - and very risky. Final episode of the French crime drama, starring Caroline Proust

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints the scene from a cabin, hidden in the middle of a plush green forest and surrounded by the sounds of nature

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Made in Britain

programme.title: Life of a Mountain: A Year on Scafell Pike

Contributions from the British Mountaineering Council and National Trust volunteers highlight the importance of maintaining the landscape for future generations

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Made in Britain
programme.desc: Fred watches a traditional chain-maker at work and visits a copper mine, before discovering how the metal sheets produced would be transformed through a spinning process. He also takes a trip to the Ffestiniog railway, built to transport slate, where he rides on the footplate of a 19th-century train and sees the world's oldest working steam engine

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross create a peaceful, dewy-brown mountain scene featuring a fresh morning lake and succulent evergreens

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits archaeological digs in the north of Britain, where finds include evidence for the first Roman siege in the UK. There's also a look at Lindisfarne, which was violently sacked by the Vikings 1,000 years ago

programme.title: Egypt's Lost Cities
programme.desc: Dallas Campbell and Liz Bonnin join Dr Sarah Parcak on a journey to Egypt to find out whether cities, temples and pyramids are lying beneath the sands. The potential existence of the buildings was suggested by satellites as part of an alternative approach to archaeology

programme.title: The Man Who Discovered Egypt
programme.desc: A profile of Victorian adventurer Flinders Petrie, who changed the world's understanding of ancient Egyptian civilisation. Chris Naunton explains how Petrie conducted a scientific survey of the pyramids, making valuable historical discoveries along the way, and also reveals how Petrie's methods of investigation revolutionised archaeology

programme.title: Craftivism: Making a Difference
programme.desc: Writer, comedian and art lover Jenny Eclair meets people doing extraordinary things with knitting, cross-stitch, banners and felt to change hearts and minds across the world

programme.desc: Delivery Duckling arrives and tells Love Monster he has been chosen to be sipping host at a special Hot Chocolate Day ceremony. She then cycles off before telling him what a sipping host does. Love Monster goes to Elder Panda to ask his advice, and he tells him there are many ways to serve hot chocolate - each person needs to find their own unique way. Elder Panda adds honey and banana from his garden to his. Love Monster starts the ceremony by adding his signature marshmallows, but then Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny announces she puts marshmallows in hers too. Love Monster thinks he needs to use something more unusual, so he adds pickles, but this doesn’t go down well - in fact, it tastes horrible! It turns out the reason why he was chosen was so that he could make hot chocolate the way he always makes it. He does, and everyone loves it.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster has been chosen as the prize-picker at the Harvest Day contest, but when he gets there it turns out there is only one prize and three teams. This means he has to choose a winner between his friends - Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny, Bad Idea Puppy and the In-Chicks. Round one is fancy fashion foodies, and everyone loves Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny, so Love Monster thinks he can just pick her. Then it is round two - the veggie variety show. Everyone loves the In-Chicks, so Love Monster now has to choose between them and Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny. Then it is the final round, which is the tantalizing taste test of home grown goodies, and this time everyone loves Bad Idea Puppy. Love Monster has no idea how he can pick a winner and runs off and hides, but then he realises Bad Idea Puppy always makes him laugh, so he declares him the winner.

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?

A microscope camera reveals the tiny prisms inside the stickers, which make them bright and shiny

programme.desc: Grandmaster Glitch has taken his teddy, Cuddles, to Ilulissat in Greenland to show him the sights, but Cuddles falls out of Grim HQ and gets stuck in the snow. The Grimbots melt the ice around Cuddles, and Glitch then uses Grim HQ to pull up the massive chunk of ice and carry it into the air. The Go Jetters are also in Ilulissat trying to spot some whales. They learn that Ilulissat means iceberg and that an iceberg is a huge chunk of ice which forms on land and then breaks off into the sea. Just then, Glitch's heavy iceberg falls from Grim HQ and starts sliding down the hill towards the town. The Go Jetters need to stop it and protect the town!

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Elder Panda is teaching Love Monster to fly a kite. Love Monster loves to watch it swish around in the air. Elder Panda goes off to get some froyo, which is perfect on Kite Day. Before he goes, he tells Love Monster that soon it will be time for him to go on to his next kiting challenge. Love Monster looks at all the other kites and sees the dragon kite, which is the largest kite of them all. Love Monster’s small kite doesn’t seem that exciting any more. He decides to try the dragon kite for just a minute. At first it seems easy, but then a wind gets it tangled in a tree. The rope breaks, and the kite flies off, causing mayhem in Fluffytown with everyone thinking it is a real dragon invasion. Finally, Elder Panda arrives and manages to bring it down to the ground using an umbrella, and Love Monster comes clean, saying how sorry he is. Elder Panda is disappointed but he does understand, as he too wanted to fly before he could run, but sometimes you have to start small before going big.

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: When renowned concert pianist Pasha Verdinikov is shot dead during an apparent burglary, Neville is quick to conclude that the scene was staged and Pasha was murdered. But the four people under suspicion - the victim's family and domestic workers - all have seemingly solid alibis. However, matters are complicated when the discovery of new evidence links the crime to a case from 20 years ago. First of a two-part story, starring Ralf Little and Josephine Jobert

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Supermarket Secrets
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace examines the ways supermarkets fight for customers' loyalty, and visits a farm claiming to be using technology to produce healthy chicken at a low cost. Elsewhere, Gregg meets Asda's head of fruit and vegetables to find out why it is a challenge to remove plastic packaging from products, and Nikki Fox sees how they are using a virtual supermarket to see what appeals to us most

programme.title: Breakfast

programme.title: Morning Live

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development progress of a former pub in Sittingbourne in Kent, a mid-terrace in Creswell, Nottinghamshire and a terraced house in Fulham, west London

programme.title: Wanted: A Simple Life

programme.title: Caught Red Handed

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: The penultimate leg of Si King and Dave Myers' European baking tour takes them to France. Travelling from Lyon to Carcassonne, they learn the secrets of teeth-pulling nougat, go truffle hunting and meet up with Dave's old flatmate, whom he has not seen for 30 years. Culinary delights en route include tarte aux pralines, a savoury-stuffed brioche, apricot tarte Tatin and, of course, the traditional French baguette

programme.desc: Live coverage of today's briefing from the Scottish Government on the coronavirus outbreak

programme.title: Garden Rescue

programme.desc: The results from Joe's second post-mortem point the finger at a new suspect, while Poppy gets an unexpected present - with upsetting implications. Jessie's effort to understand Amber backfire badly

programme.desc: Mary Berry challenges the celebrity cooks to prepare the perfect breakfast for houseguests. programme.title: Do Black Lives Matter in Brazil?
programme.desc: In the year when Black Lives Matter became a rallying cry around the world, a BBC Brazil investigation discovered that in the first half of 2020, more people were killed by the police in the state of Rio de Janeiro state than in the whole of the USA. Of those killed, 75% were black Brazilians, many of whom were just teenagers. Ligia Guimaraes meets several families fighting for justice over the killing of their sons.

programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: More celebrity interviews and topical debate from a female perspective

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Lingo
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Adil Ray in which couples from Marple and Darlington and flatmates from Cheadle use their powers of deduction to work out words that appear in the Lingo grids and Puzzlewords. The last remaining duo have the opportunity to win the money they've banked earlier in the show to potentially double their winnings, or leaving with nothing

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Dechlan, Marryk, Jess and Trudi pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mack sets a trap, Nicola and Jimmy are shocked, and Harriet makes a decision

programme.title: South Africa with Gregg Wallace
programme.desc: The MasterChef presenter takes a tuk tuk ride to explore the colourful and energetic township of Soweto just outside Johannesburg, home of both Desmond Tutu and Nelson Mandela. He dishes up lunch in a school canteen and learns the lively gum boot dance

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Home Sweet Home - Travels in My Own Land
programme.desc: After a lifetime of travels that have taken her across the globe, Joanna Lumley is making her most personal journey yet in the UK. Starting at Tilbury Docks in Essex she heads north, making a nostalgic return to Coronation Street, before ending the journey in Whitby

programme.title: Marcella
programme.desc: Rav discovers Marcella is working undercover. Jack is released from prison and rejoins the Maguire family. The fallout from Jessie's death ripples through the community. Marcella's mental state begins to suffer as she discovers someone is on to her

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Unbelievable Moments Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Alastair Stewart presents a selection of extraordinary eyewitness footage filmed by members of the public, featuring a wedding that was ruined by a sandstorm, the rescue of a fox cub from a drain, cyclists being chased by an ostrich, a train that crashed into a truck at a level crossing, a paraglider's painful crash landing, and a musical moment that raised thousands for charity and started an 80-year-old grandfather's recording career

programme.title: The Bay
programme.desc: Determined to ignore Andy's unexpected arrival the night before, Lisa throws herself into the case and discovers a significant lead from her only eyewitness. The new information she attains opens up the investigation and the team uncover vital evidence, forcing them to deal with a difficult suspect. Crime drama, starring Morven Christie and Daniel Ryan

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Entertainment news

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: With the support of fiance Michael Brown, Preston mum Roisin Pelan is facing stage 3C cancer. Despite enduring challenging treatment, she has raised thousands for charities and is determined to see young daughter Ivy grow up. Alan Titchmarsh, David Domoney, Frances Tophill and Katie Rushworth proceed to turn their dangerous sloping garden into a stunning music festival-inspired haven

Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Hilary, Dickie, Eleanor and Paddy pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jamie unwittingly meets the imposter client, Paul needs cash fast, and there is a slight thawing between Debbie and Charity

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Dev is stunned when Asha reveals that she has a date with Nina. When Paul reveals to Todd that Will was responsible for wrecking the flat, Grimshaw pretends to be shocked. When Aadi announces that he'll be conducting Evelyn's performance review at lunchtime, she's seriously unimpressed

programme.title: Cornwall and Devon Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter is in South Devon taking on a section of the South West Coast Path from the affluent coastal town of Salcombe to rugged Prawle Point. Here, she samples the world famous Salcombe crab, rambles the coast in search of wildlife with naturalist Claire, and meets one of the volunteers of the National Coast watch, who helps prevent shipwrecks on this dramatic and stunning stretch of coast

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Paul's anger turns to despair as Billy asserts that he has to put Summer first. Dev does his best to make Nina feel welcome at his house. Simon fears that without his stolen bike he'll lose his job

programme.title: The Bay
programme.desc: As the team press on with the investigation, Lisa and Med dig deeper into the family and discover not all relationships with the victim were amicable. With one suspect beginning to talk, a crucial piece of evidence is brought to light, leaving Lisa with more questions than answers. Meanwhile, Andy continues to grow closer to Abbie and Rob. Starring Morven Christie and Daniel Ryan

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Home Sweet Home - Travels in My Own Land
programme.desc: After a lifetime of travels that have taken her across the globe, Joanna Lumley is making her most personal journey yet in the UK. Starting at Tilbury Docks in Essex she heads north, making a nostalgic return to Coronation Street, before ending the journey in Whitby

programme.title: Unbelievable Moments Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Alastair Stewart presents a selection of extraordinary eyewitness footage filmed by members of the public, featuring a wedding that was ruined by a sandstorm, the rescue of a fox cub from a drain, cyclists being chased by an ostrich, a train that crashed into a truck at a level crossing, a paraglider's painful crash landing, and a musical moment that raised thousands for charity and started an 80-year-old grandfather's recording career

programme.title: The Bay
programme.desc: Determined to ignore Andy's unexpected arrival the night before, Lisa throws herself into the case and discovers a significant lead from her only eyewitness. programme.desc: With the support of fiance Michael Brown, Preston mum Roisin Pelan is facing stage 3C cancer. programme.desc: Meg is thrilled to be recruited as a model - but not everything is as it seems with the job. Meanwhile, Brian accompanies Stewie on a road trip adventure when the baby decides he wants to experience life outside of preschool

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Part one of two. The Griffins find themselves stuck in Springfield, home to the Simpsons, after fleeing Quahog when Peter faces a backlash over a misogynistic comic strip

programme.title: Plebs
programme.desc: Grumio finds an abandoned baby behind the bins while doing the recycling and takes it home to raise as his own, but Stylax uses the infant to strike up a relationship with an attractive young wet nurse. Marcus tries to avoid seeing Cynthia's painfully boring one-woman play. Comedy, starring Tom Rosenthal, Joel Fry and Ryan Sampson

programme.title: Plebs
programme.desc: Grumio falls ill after eating an apple while on the toilet, but Marcus refuses to pay for his medical treatment - preferring instead to go with Cynthia's remedy of candlelight and prayer. Meanwhile, Stylax strikes up a rapport with rich nobleman Gaius, who agrees to fund his career in chariot racing. Comedy, guest starring James Fleet (The Vicar of Dibley)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Conclusion of the story in which the Griffin family are stuck in Springfield, home to the Simpsons, after Peter creates a controversial comic strip

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Jeff wins a tour through a mysterious weed factory and brings Stan along. Animated comedy about a man who will stop at nothing to protect his country

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Hayley finds herself pushed aside when Jeff's racing greyhound Fleabiscuit wins the Chimdale Stakes. Klaus, believing that the mechanical rabbit Wizbo is too plain looking, sets out to give it a little more personality and ends up going for a wild ride

programme.title: The Stand Up Sketch Show
programme.desc: Routines by comedians including Bobby Mair, Judi Love, Catherine Bohart and Rhys Nicholson are reconstructed as sketches

programme.title: The Stand Up Sketch Show
programme.desc: Routines by comedians including Marlon Davis, Russell Kane, Luke McQueen and Seann Walsh are reconstructed as sketches

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Amy turns a boring Christmas Eve at Cloud 9 into a holiday adventure. Meanwhile, Jonah and Garrett's roommate issues boil over

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Dina and Cheyenne snoop through the staff members' social media feeds after a controversial video is posted. Glenn suspects Mateo is guilty of fraud

programme.title: Iain Stirling's CelebAbility
programme.desc: Presenters Joe Swash and Maya Jama, Emmerdale's Laura Norton, runner Jonnie Peacock and comedian David Morgan take on five friends in a series of challenges

programme.title: Totally Bonkers Guinness World Records
programme.desc: Matt Edmondson narrates a selection of unusual records, including the world's smallest horse, the world's fastest human crab, and the highest note ever sung

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Zoe has a drunken night out with Leanne. Natalie books a holiday for her and Kevin. Judy panics when the midwife catches her rowing with Zoe

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Chris is surprised when Angie tells him she will move in with him. Deirdre tries to calm Jon down about Linda taking the house. Mavis plans to leave the street

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Biff receives an unexpected gift from Tara. Steve receives a mysterious phone call. Kim receives the results of James' paternity test

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Betty struggles to cope with Kathy's new American menu. Kim breaks the bad news to the Glovers. Marlon warns Paddy about lending any money to the Dingles

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Rivalry between two brass bands gets out of hand and a musician dies of suspected poisoning. Dawn is swept off her feet by a handsome stranger, leaving Gina less than happy when she announces she is moving to Paris with him. Joe and Rachel succumb to their attraction, only for Carol to catch them kissing. Rural drama, guest starring Keith Barron and Peter Wight

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid informs Jean he has been given a temporary promotion at work, but she soon begins to doubt what he says. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: The Ropers babysit for their neighbours, but George misses his usual routine - so he nips out to a darts match with the infant in tow. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The detective's god-daughter Lydia and her son James - who is severely traumatised by a bombing at his school - come to stay unexpectedly. Struggling to look after the lad when his mother goes missing, Foyle's life suffers a further complication when the body of a local man is found near a military centre - leading him into the top-secret world of weapons research. Kate Fleetwood and Michael Jayston guest star, with Michael Kitchen and Honeysuckle Weeks

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mason is called to help Evelyn, a widow who is receiving threats from a corrupt associate of her late husband. Her son-in-law Alan is reluctant to sever his ties with the crook, knowing he provides vital revenue - which forces her into silence. But the police urge Evelyn to cooperate after her car is run off the road and her grandson goes missing. Meanwhile, a Polish visitor arrives in search of a long-lost love

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy stalls over lending Mandy the money. Kim tells Tara she is pathetic for marrying Michael. Steve tells Kim to consider his plan

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Steve tries to persuade Kim to go ahead with the robbery. Tara shows a prospective buyer around the stud farm. Zoe offers Paddy a partnership, but it will cost him 45,000 pounds

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Judy makes plans to give up working to look after Katie. Mavis and Rita make plans for their bed and breakfast idea. Alan and Fiona go out for a meal with his parents

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Mallets tell the midwife that Zoe has disappeared and they do not know where she is. Alec attempts to put Rita off the idea of moving with Mavis. Chris comforts an upset Sally

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Sergeant Troy is promoted to Inspector, but before he can consider his future he has to help Barnaby investigate the collapse of a tunnel which has exposed a collection of ancient skeletons - and more recent remains among the bones suggest links to a nearby double shooting. Murder mystery, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Cherie Lunghi and David Bradley

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A prominent bank manager is almost killed by a hit-and-run driver just before he is due to set sail on the luxury liner Queen Mary. Convinced his life is in danger, he persuades Poirot to accompany him - and his valuable shipment of liberty bonds - to New York. But the Belgian sleuth's main concern is his own terrible sea-sickness. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The Cornish copper looks into a lone yachtswoman's mysterious disappearance. He learns she had been receiving threats from the owners of a local china clay works - where he later makes a grisly discovery. Jack Shepherd stars, with Con O'Neill and Michael Attwell

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A socialite is found dead next to the swimming pool of her manor house - following a series of threats against her. Barnaby and Troy are forced to dive deep into a dark world of adultery and deceit in affluent Midsomer Malham when they investigate. Mystery, starring John Nettles and Daniel Casey, with Charles Collingwood, Ann Bell and Miles Richardson

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When an RAF officer is found hanged in a wood, Meredith - the new chief superintendent at Hastings - is quick to dismiss it as a straightforward suicide. However, an aerial photo of a German village found in the dead man's pocket leads Milner to suspect murder. The newly retired Foyle is compelled to return to service when events take an even more unexpected turn. Wartime crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Feature-length episode of the crime drama. DI Frost has his hands full coping with death threats, arson and a staff shortage at the station - but anything is better than staying at home with an unwelcome guest. David Jason stars as the grouchy inspector, with Tony Haygarth, Diane Bull, Denise Black and Dominic Jephcott

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean worries about strange noises coming from next door. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James as the head of the family, struggling to keep his wife and children in tow

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Eagle-eyed George sees an investment opportunity when Mildred suggests their dog should have puppies. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When an RAF officer is found hanged in a wood, Meredith - the new chief superintendent at Hastings - is quick to dismiss it as a straightforward suicide. However, an aerial photo of a German village found in the dead man's pocket leads Milner to suspect murder. The newly retired Foyle is compelled to return to service when events take an even more unexpected turn. Wartime crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Dawson helps the officers investigate antiques dealer Mr Brigstocke, who is accused of selling stolen goods. Mason and Wetherby stake out the shop to catch him red-handed, but the situation becomes life-threatening when Brigstocke sets fire to it. Meanwhile, prejudices are stirred by the arrival of some Taoist monks and tragedy strikes local troublemakers Julie and Eddie Tinniswood. With Clare Wille and Joe McFadden

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe and Paddy discuss his partnership plans. Mandy finishes with Paddy when she finds out he is going to let down the Dingles. Sarah is not happy with Ned's parenting advice

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy is upset about her break-up with Paddy. Steve worries about the trial run of the robbery. Kathy tells Tara to leave Biff alone

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera tells Judy she is a good woman for taking on Zoe's baby. Mavis and Rita have a disappointing time while house hunting. Chris tells Sally that Kevin was a fool to lose her

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Des is upset that Ritchie is back in Samantha's life. Mavis gets Rita to put an offer in on the Cartmel house. Fiona tells Maxine that she thinks she is pregnant

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A local woman is found murdered following a row with her husband at a residents' association Spanish-themed evening. The finger of suspicion soon points to an odd-job man with a violent history, and the chairman of the association reveals he heard a squeaking noise after the victim left - but the witness promptly winds up dead himself. Crime drama, starring John Nettles

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth is asked to vet potential suitors vying for the hand of an Australian heiress and discreetly accompanies her on a rail journey aboard the Plymouth Express, only to find himself investigating a jewel theft. Mystery, with David Suchet and Julian Wadham

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A dark family secret comes to light when a female hit-and-run suspect claims the woman she deliberately ran over on a pedestrian crossing was a ghost. However, the facts of the case tell a different story. Gwen Taylor guest stars, with Jack Shepherd

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth finds himself in interesting company as English socialites and American millionaires rub shoulders on board a train bound for the French Riviera. Passengers include oil magnate Rufus Van Aldin (Elliott Gould) and his daughter Ruth (Jaime Murray) - to whom he has given a magnificent ruby known as the Heart of Fire. With members of the aristocracy crammed into a confined space with their husbands, lovers and a renowned gemstone, murder cannot be far away. With Lindsay Duncan, Roger Lloyd Pack and James D'Arcy

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When an unpopular employee at a military psychiatric hospital is found murdered, there is no shortage of suspects, but a colleague of the man, an exiled Polish Jew, holds himself responsible for the death. Foyle is convinced there is another explanation, but his priorities shift when news of a missing boy from London reaches Hastings. Starring Michael Kitchen, Phyllida Law and Nicholas Woodeson

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The detective is paired with DC Webster, an officer who is struggling to fit in with life in Denton after being seconded from Scotland, with the duo investigating a robbery at a local casino and a hit-and-run incident involving Roger Massie, the son of a local politician, and soon finds that both matters are linked. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander and George Anton

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is worried about an intruder, and wonders whether to scare the man off or call the police

programme.desc: Text-based information service
